text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy county bordering a dense forest, there was a charming little village called Pineville. It was famous for its ancient oak tree cemetery filled with poets and painters whose spirits linger on painting roses among the oaks.

In Pineville lived a quiet girl named Olive. She loved ancient lore and loved listening to the old ladies gossip about thewoods near old Oakwoods Manor. One cloudy afternoon, while hiding behind an oak, she learned the manor owned an extraordinary cat.

The cat was gray as owl feathers with ruby eyes. Every night, she'd leap onto Oakcliffe terrace. Olive listened till dawn. Oakcliffe spoke fluent Owlgleaston rusty.

"What splendid birds they are," Oakcliffe said dreamily. "You should hear them talk about the moons. Or perchance, your dreams of them."

Olive was enchanted. "Where do you go?"

"Just up Windham Ridge. You wouldn't like it."

"Why?"

"Alix does. She loves them."
